payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Pocket money task assigned to children
Task completion record tracked for compensation
Enable children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks on a weekly basis
Provide children visibility into available and completed task inventory
Weekly aggregation of earned amounts for child presentation
Aggregated reporting of child earnings and task completion metrics for parent consumption

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming process initiated by children
payload.justification: Task claiming is a strategic process capability enabling child engagement with the system.

payload.description: Task assignment definition process performed by parents
payload.justification: Task assignment creation is a strategic process enabling parental task management.

payload.description: Compensation distribution process based on child performance
payload.justification: Compensation distribution is a strategic process requiring accurate performance data.

payload.description: Task completion record assigned to children for pocket money compensation
payload.justification: Task is a core strategic entity referenced across claiming, assignment definition, and inventory visibility signals. | Completed task state is a distinct entity implied by tracking and inventory visibility requirements.

payload.description: Weekly earnings summary for child users
payload.justification: Earnings summary is a strategic data entity required for child financial visibility.

payload.description: Child performance report for parental review
payload.justification: Performance report is a strategic entity enabling parental oversight and compensation decision-making.

payload.description: Longitudinal task history supporting accountability analysis
payload.justification: Task history is a strategic data entity required for trend and accountability purposes.

payload.description: Monetary value associated with task assignment
payload.justification: Monetary value is a strategic attribute of task assignments enabling compensation calculation.

payload.description: Weekly task claiming and tracking cycle
payload.justification: Weekly periodicity is a strategic temporal cycle governing task management operations.

payload.description: Weekly earnings summary generation cycle
payload.justification: Weekly earnings reporting represents a strategic temporal cycle for financial visibility.

payload.description: Child user claiming and tracking pocket money tasks
payload.justification: Child actor is implicit across signals describing task claiming, task visibility, and earnings viewing capabilities.

payload.description: Parent user defining tasks and accessing performance reports
payload.justification: Parent actor is implicit in signals describing task assignment definition and performance reporting access.

payload.description: Enable children to view available tasks and track completed pocket money tasks on a weekly basis
payload.justification: SG049 expresses a fundamental strategic purpose directly aligned with child task management capability. | SG051 captures the strategic purpose of task transparency for child users.

payload.description: Enable parents to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values
payload.justification: SG050 articulates parental motivation for task and compensation configuration as a strategic goal.

payload.description: Enable children to view their weekly earnings summary
payload.justification: SG052 identifies the motivational goal of earnings visibility as a strategic driver.

payload.description: Enable parents to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution
payload.justification: SG053 establishes parental oversight and payment accuracy as a strategic need.

payload.description: Provide longitudinal task history access supporting accountability and trend analysis
payload.justification: SG054 indicates stakeholder requirement for historical data access as a strategic capability driver.

payload.description: Task claiming by child
payload.justification: SG290 establishes claiming as a business process whereby children associate themselves with completed tasks.

payload.description: Task specification with monetary value
payload.justification: SG291 defines the parent capability to create and configure task entities with reward values.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregation
payload.justification: SG294 describes the business function of calculating and displaying cumulative weekly earnings totals.

payload.description: Parent reporting of child earnings
payload.justification: SG295 defines the reporting function that displays child-task-earnings data to support payment obligations.

payload.description: Task visibility filtering by status and temporal scope
payload.justification: SG293 describes the function of presenting different task views to children based on availability and completion within time boundaries.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset
payload.justification: SG296 establishes a cyclical business rule governing when task availability is refreshed.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained and accessible
payload.justification: SG297 mandates a data retention rule ensuring task history persists across time periods.

payload.description: Task
payload.justification: Task is a core business entity referenced across multiple signals, representing units of work that children complete for reward.

payload.description: Monetary value of task
payload.justification: SG291 explicitly defines monetary value as an attribute associated with task entities.

payload.description: Task completion status
payload.justification: Signals distinguish between available and completed tasks, indicating completion status as a key attribute.

payload.description: Child claims completed task
payload.justification: SG290 and SG292 establish the core relationship between child entities and completed task entities through claiming/linkage.

payload.description: Parent specifies task
payload.justification: SG291 defines the relationship between parent actors and task entities through specification capability.

payload.description: Child-task-earnings association
payload.justification: SG295 explicitly describes a three-way relationship linking children, tasks, and earnings for reporting purposes.

payload.description: Task completion
payload.justification: Task completion is a significant business event that triggers claiming and earnings tracking processes.

payload.description: Weekly temporal period
payload.justification: Weekly cycle is the fundamental timing constraint governing earnings aggregation and task availability reset.

payload.description: Weekly boundary reached for task reset
payload.justification: SG296 specifies a temporal trigger that initiates the task availability reset business rule at weekly intervals.

payload.description: Child
payload.justification: Child is a primary business actor who claims tasks, views availability, and monitors earnings.

payload.description: Parent
payload.justification: Parent is a primary business actor who specifies tasks and reviews child earnings for payment fulfillment.

payload.description: Track children's earned monetary rewards for completed tasks
payload.justification: The core business goal driving the system is monitoring and aggregating children's earnings from task completion.

payload.description: Support parent payment fulfillment to children
payload.justification: SG295 explicitly states the purpose of parent reporting is to enable payment obligation fulfillment.

payload.description: Child visibility into task opportunities and completion history
payload.justification: SG293 establishes the principle that children should have transparent access to both available and completed tasks within scope.

payload.description: Historical data preservation for temporal analysis
payload.justification: SG297 mandates retention of historical task data, reflecting a principle of maintaining temporal continuity for business insight.

payload.description: Earnings must aggregate within weekly temporal boundaries
payload.justification: The weekly cycle constrains how earnings are calculated and when task availability resets, limiting business operational flexibility.

payload.description: Task completion event triggers linkage to child entity records
payload.justification: SG457 describes a logical association rule governing the relationship between task completion events and child entities, expressed as a design-level constraint on data linkage behavior.

payload.description: Child user access to task collections is governed by visibility constraints
payload.justification: SG458 specifies logical access patterns as a rule defining data visibility requirements for child users accessing task collections.

payload.description: Aggregated reporting of child earnings and task completion metrics presented weekly for parent consumption
payload.justification: SG459 describes temporal aggregation logic as a computational function that processes and presents weekly earned amounts to children. | SG460 defines a logical reporting interface as a function providing parents with aggregated views of child activity data.

payload.description: Weekly reset cycle for task availability restoration
payload.justification: SG461 specifies periodic state reset logic as a weekly cycle event governing task availability behavior.

payload.description: Historical task records must be logically persisted and accessible
payload.justification: SG462 mandates data retention as a design constraint requiring logical persistence and accessibility of historical task data.

payload.description: Operating system version compatibility requirement for deployment platform
payload.justification: Signal SG530 describes a normative platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete OS versions for deployment. At the Builder level, this represents a technical constraint governing which operating system versions are permitted for system deployment. Confidence matches source signal (0.85).

payload.description: Weekly earned amount total becomes accessible to child actors
payload.justification: SG542 establishes a weekly operational schedule boundary defining when access occurs. Classified as Event representing a specific timing specification at Row 5 detailed sequencing level.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset cycle timing specification
payload.justification: SG544 specifies weekly operational cycle timing for task reset operations. Classified as Cycle at Row 5 representing detailed recurring temporal sequencing specification.

payload.description: iOS mobile platform deployment node
payload.justification: SG545 specifies iOS as a distinct deployment platform location. Classified as Node at Row 5 representing detailed deployment specification. Part of named instance group for three platform nodes. | SG545 specifies Android as a distinct deployment platform location. Classified as Node at Row 5 representing detailed deployment specification. Part of named instance group for three platform nodes.

payload.description: Windows desktop platform deployment node
payload.justification: SG545 specifies Windows as a distinct deployment platform location. Classified as Node at Row 5 representing detailed deployment specification. Part of named instance group for three platform nodes.

payload.description: Child user actor executing task claiming operations
payload.justification: SG539 explicitly identifies children as operational actors performing task claiming activities. Classified as Actor at Row 5 representing detailed user interface specification. Part of named instance group distinguishing Child from Parent actors.

payload.description: Parent user actor performing task configuration and value assignment operations
payload.justification: SG540 explicitly identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task setup and monetary assignment. Classified as Actor at Row 5 representing detailed user interface specification. Part of named instance group distinguishing Parent from Child actors.

payload.description: Child role with operational visibility rights to view available and completed task lists
payload.justification: SG541 defines visibility requirements for child actors, representing role-based access specifications at the detailed interface level. Classified as Role rather than Actor because it specifies access rights and operational capabilities.

payload.description: Parent role with operational access rights to view reports for pocket money distribution
payload.justification: SG543 defines parent operational role in report access and distribution activities, representing role-based interface specifications at the detailed level. Classified as Role to capture access and responsibility specifications.

payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.
